Mosch..personality: A self-willed, obstinate, and selfish constitutional type, typically developed through a lifetime of indulgence where every whim was gratified from infancy. These individuals are crafty and cunning manipulators who produce elaborate displays of symptoms to overwhelm those around them into submission.
Mosch..mental_state: The mind is dominated by hysterical perversion, producing erratic and unpredictable neuropathic phenomena that defy conventional clinical assessment. There is a fundamental unreliability in self-reporting, as the patient has traded upon sensations and imagination for so long that truthful statements become impossible. Constant hurry, dropping things, foolish gestures, and a dread of death with incessant talk of dying characterize the mental picture.
Mosch..emotional_pattern: Violent fits of anger with rage and scolding escalate until the face turns blue and the patient faints, alternating with states of anguish, apprehension, trembling, and palpitation. Peevishness and quarrelsomeness dominate interpersonal relations, with a deep-seated fear of death including fear of lying down lest she die. The emotional life is fundamentally performative and manipulative, with symptoms intensifying in both quantity and intensity until all desires are attained.
